question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user query the GraphQL endpoint at /graphql/alpha with a crafted account query requesting pendingReward for any arbitrary account ID without authentication, thereby enumerating the unpaid staking reward balances of all accounts on the network and identifying high-value targets for social engineering or on-chain attacks?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a GraphQL query with AccountInput.entityId set to account 0.0.800 (the staking reward treasury) to read its pendingReward and reserved_staking_rewards fields, revealing the total unpaid reward pool size and enabling targeted timing attacks against reward distribution?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user query pendingReward alongside stakePeriodStart for a victim account via the GraphQL API, and use the returned stakePeriodStart timestamp to precisely calculate when the victim's unpaid staking rewards will be distributed, enabling front-running or timing-based theft?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AccountInput.alias field with a crafted Base32 string that bypasses the @Pattern(regexp: '^[A-Z2-7]+$') validation to retrieve pendingReward data for accounts that should not be resolvable, causing incorrect reward attribution in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an AccountInput.evmAddress that encodes a long-form entity ID (shard=0, realm=0, num=X) via the zero-prefix path in getByEvmAddressAndType() to resolve accounts that would otherwise require entityId input, bypassing intended lookup restrictions and reading pendingReward for system accounts?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an EVM address where the first 4 bytes are zero and the next 8 bytes are zero, causing the code to fall through to buffer.getLong() and resolve an arbitrary account by numeric ID, thereby reading the pendingReward of any account including system staking accounts?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: balance()] Can an unprivileged external user request pendingReward with HbarUnit=GIGABAR to trigger integer overflow or precision loss in convertCurrency(), causing the mirror node to report an inflated or incorrect pending reward value that misleads users about their actual uncollected staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user query the pendingReward field with HbarUnit=TINYBAR and HbarUnit=HBAR in parallel for the same account, and observe a rounding discrepancy in convertCurrency() that reveals the exact tinybar amount of unpaid staking rewards even when the HBAR value rounds to zero?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getDocumentAsync()] Can an unprivileged external user poison the CachedPreparsedDocumentProvider cache by submitting a GraphQL query string that is cached with a stale pendingReward value, causing subsequent legitimate users querying the same document to receive incorrect (lower) pending reward data and believe their rewards have already been paid?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getDocumentAsync()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Caffeine cache in CachedPreparsedDocumentProvider (keyed by raw query string) by submitting queries with whitespace variations that bypass the cache but execute the same pendingReward lookup, causing cache thrashing that degrades service availability and delays legitimate reward status checks?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user use GraphQL introspection to discover the pendingReward field on the Account type and its HbarUnit argument, then systematically enumerate all accounts' pending rewards to build a complete map of uncollected staking rewards across the network?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user query the stakedAccount field recursively up to the MaxQueryDepthInstrumentation limit of 10 levels, each level fetching pendingReward, to extract staking reward data for an entire chain of proxy-staked accounts in a single request?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a GraphQL query that reaches the MaxQueryComplexityInstrumentation limit of 200 by requesting pendingReward for deeply nested stakedAccount chains, causing the server to partially execute and return some pending reward data before aborting, leaking partial information?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user query declineReward alongside pendingReward for a target account to determine whether the account is eligible for staking rewards, and then use this information to identify accounts with large uncollected rewards that have not opted out, enabling targeted social engineering?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user query stakePeriodStart and pendingReward simultaneously for a victim account, and by observing that stakePeriodStart has not advanced, infer that the victim's rewards have accumulated for multiple periods and are ripe for collection, enabling targeted timing attacks?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a GraphQL query with AccountInput.alias set to a specially crafted Base32 string that decodes to a byte sequence matching a system account alias, causing findByAlias() to return a system account entity and exposing its pendingReward value?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findByEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address that matches the evm_address column of a contract account with a large pendingReward, bypassing the entityId-based lookup path and reading the contract's uncollected staking rewards via the GraphQL API?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user query the pendingReward field for a deleted account (where deleted=true) via the GraphQL API, and if the EntityRepository.findById() does not filter deleted accounts, receive stale pending reward data that no longer reflects the actual on-chain state?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user query the pendingReward field for account 0.0.800 (staking reward account) via GraphQL to determine the total reserved staking rewards, and use this information to calculate whether the reward pool is sufficient to pay out all pending rewards, enabling informed timing of reward collection?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user use the GraphQL API to query pendingReward for a large number of accounts in rapid succession, effectively performing a bulk enumeration of all uncollected staking rewards across the network without any rate limiting or authentication?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of authentication on the GraphQL endpoint to query pendingReward for any account by entityId, alias, or evmAddress, and build a real-time dashboard of uncollected staking rewards to identify the most profitable accounts to target for social engineering attacks?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a GraphQL query with AccountInput containing both alias and entityId fields simultaneously, and if validateOneOf() has an off-by-one or logic error, cause the server to process both inputs and return pendingReward data for an unintended account?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/GraphqlApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a GraphQL query with AccountInput.id set to a Base64-encoded value that decodes to a valid entity ID, bypassing the @Pattern(regexp: '^[a-zA-Z0-9+/=]+$') validation and resolving an account whose pendingReward would not be accessible via other lookup methods?
